title: opencode-ghost
note: Canonical ghost-spec repository for source-isolated reimplementation.

title: opencode source implementation
note: The original opencode codebase is the first certified consumer of promoted ghost artifacts.

title: canonical contract graph
note: The sole writable semantic index for surfaces, artifacts, claims, worlds, and promotion state.

title: contract graph schema
note: JSON Schema for each JSONL row in the canonical contract graph.

title: extractor script
note: Deterministic extractor for inventories and file-level traceability.

attrs.minimality: The inventories are not otherwise derivable without an extractor.

title: governance render script
note: Generator for governance views derived from the contract graph.

attrs.minimality: Generated ledgers require one canonical renderer.

title: graph and artifact validation script
note: Validation script for JSON artifacts, graph references, and required paths.

attrs.minimality: The graph requires one canonical integrity checker.

title: runtime commuting script
note: Machine-checks the permission-cycle commuting diagram for the runtime witness slice.

attrs.minimality: The runtime commutativity witness needs one executable projection checker.

title: runtime certification script
note: Builds the runtime-formal source-first certification transcript.

attrs.minimality: The certification transcript must be generated canonically.

title: TUI commuting script
note: Machine-checks the commuting diagrams for the certified TUI case set.

attrs.minimality: The TUI commutativity witness set needs one executable projection checker.

title: TUI certification script
note: Builds the TUI-certified source-first certification transcripts for the promoted case set.

attrs.minimality: The TUI certification transcripts must be generated canonically.

title: verify-all entrypoint
note: Pinned verification entrypoint invoking all phase hooks in deterministic order.

attrs.minimality: One canonical verifier entrypoint is required for reproducibility.

title: normalization law checker
note: Validates determinism, idempotence, monotonic erasure, and semantic preservation for runtime and TUI normalizers.

attrs.minimality: Normalization laws require one executable checker to be release-bearing.

title: determinism checker
note: Checks that generated inventories, reports, evidence, and certification transcripts are byte-stable across regeneration.

attrs.minimality: Deterministic regeneration needs one canonical checker.

title: semantic freeze report generator
note: Generates the bootstrap semantic-freeze report from graph coverage and certification state.

attrs.minimality: Freeze readiness requires one canonical report generator.

title: runtime normalization tests
note: Adversarial runtime normalization fixtures covering erasure and semantic preservation.

attrs.minimality: These fixtures are the smallest runtime cases that distinguish safe erasure from over-normalization.

title: TUI normalization tests
note: Adversarial TUI normalization fixtures covering transient-style erasure and semantic preservation.

attrs.minimality: These fixtures are the smallest TUI cases that distinguish safe erasure from over-normalization.

title: artifact classification policy
note: Every tracked artifact is normative, informative, or generated.

title: promotion-state policy
note: Every normative candidate advances through draft, provisional, normative, or deprecated.

title: core-runtime
note: Runtime routes, state transitions, tools, replay, and CLI obligations sufficient for baseline certification.

title: runtime-formal
note: Formal kernel obligations over runtime semantics, including Quint and Lean artifacts.

title: tui-certified
note: Certified TUI interaction, layout, and frame obligations over the approved terminal matrix.

title: integrations-canonical
note: Canonical provider and plugin integration obligations with replay-first authority.

title: security-critical
note: Security-sensitive obligations such as permission gating, path safety, and unsafe-input rejection.

title: full-reference
note: The maximal adjudication profile: runtime, formal, TUI, integrations, and security.

title: runtime surface
note: Session, sync, permission, tool, replay, project, and worktree semantics.

title: TUI surface
note: Built-in terminal UI semantics, interaction, layout, and certified frame projections.

title: integration surface
note: Provider, plugin, capture, replay, and live-evidence boundaries.

title: security surface
note: Cross-cutting security-sensitive obligations over runtime, TUI, and integrations.

title: semantic kernel
note: Minimal kernel containing session state, event application, permission state, replay semantics, and project/worktree identity.

title: runtime witness Quint model
note: Executable Quint witness for the session create -> busy -> permission ask -> permission approve/reject runtime slices.

attrs.minimality: Captures the minimal executable kernel witness needed for model-checked runtime alignment.

title: runtime witness Lean proof
note: Lean witness for kernel replay coherence and approval/rejection final-state proofs.

attrs.minimality: Provides the irreducible proof-bearing counterpart to the Quint witness for the same kernel slice.

title: runtime permission-cycle case
note: Canonical runtime-formal case for the permission witness slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal case artifact that binds the witness slice to a concrete observation contract.

title: runtime permission-cycle commuting evidence
note: Generated evidence that the witness case, kernel projection, formal markers, and expected observation commute for the permission slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the smallest machine-checked commutativity witness for the runtime-formal profile.

title: opencode source runtime-formal transcript
note: Conformance-kit transcript certifying the opencode source tree as the first runtime-formal consumer for the permission witness slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal protocol transcript needed to represent source-first certification as a checked artifact.

title: runtime permission-reject case
note: Canonical runtime-formal case for the rejected-idle permission witness slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal rejection-path case artifact that binds the witness slice to a concrete observation contract.

title: runtime permission-reject commuting evidence
note: Generated evidence that the rejection witness case, kernel projection, formal markers, and expected observation commute for the rejected-idle slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the smallest machine-checked commutativity witness for the rejected permission path.

title: opencode source runtime-formal reject transcript
note: Conformance-kit transcript certifying the opencode source tree as the first runtime-formal consumer for the rejection witness slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal protocol transcript needed to represent source-first certification for the rejected permission path.

title: runtime workspace-lifecycle case
note: Canonical core-runtime case for the experimental workspace lifecycle route set.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal route-projection case artifact that widens core-runtime beyond the permission witness.

title: runtime workspace-lifecycle commuting evidence
note: Generated evidence that the workspace-lifecycle route projection commutes with the extracted route inventory.

attrs.minimality: This is the smallest machine-checked commuting witness for workspace lifecycle coverage.

title: opencode source core-runtime workspace-lifecycle transcript
note: Conformance-kit transcript certifying the opencode source tree for the workspace-lifecycle route slice.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal protocol transcript needed to represent source-first certification for workspace lifecycle.

title: minimal repo world
note: Smallest baseline workspace sufficient for opencode baseline certification.

title: permission-request session world
note: Canonical world for permission ask/reply and TUI permission projection.

title: source-first certification
note: No promoted artifact becomes normative until opencode itself satisfies the relevant profile under the conformance kit.

title: stable IDs are mandatory
note: Every materially relevant entity receives a permanent graph ID that is never recycled.

title: governance views are generated
note: Ledgers, matrices, and reports are generated from the graph and are not independently authoritative.

title: pending permission implies waiting phase
note: The runtime witness preserves the invariant that pending permission state and waiting permission phase coincide.

attrs.minimality: This invariant is not derivable from inventories or schemas alone; it constrains kernel behavior directly.

title: approved permission implies idle phase
note: The runtime witness preserves the invariant that approved permission replies return the session to idle.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal release-bearing postcondition for the permission witness slice.

title: rejected permission implies idle phase
note: The runtime witness preserves the invariant that rejected permission replies return the session to idle.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal release-bearing postcondition for the rejected permission witness slice.

title: permission witness replay reaches approved idle state
note: The canonical create -> busy -> ask -> approve witness converges on the approved idle kernel state.

attrs.minimality: This witness is the smallest seven-layer seed for runtime-formal alignment.

title: reject witness replay reaches rejected idle state
note: The canonical create -> busy -> ask -> reject witness converges on the rejected idle kernel state.

attrs.minimality: This witness is the smallest rejection-path seed for runtime-formal alignment.

title: permission witness projection commutes
note: The canonical permission-cycle case projects to the same kernel observation across witness steps, runtime case expectations, and formal witness markers.

attrs.minimality: This is the minimal mechanically checked commuting diagram for the runtime witness slice.

title: opencode source is certified for the permission witness slice
note: The original opencode source tree satisfies the runtime-formal witness slice under the conformance-kit protocol transcript.

attrs.minimality: This is the smallest source-first certification claim that closes the baseline witness loop.

title: runtime normalization laws hold
note: Runtime normalization satisfies determinism, idempotence, monotonic erasure, and semantic preservation on adversarial fixtures.

attrs.minimality: Normalization cannot be trusted from prose alone; it needs executable law checks.

note: TUI normalization satisfies determinism, idempotence, monotonic erasure, and semantic preservation on adversarial fixtures.

attrs.minimality: Certified TUI parity depends on a checked normalization quotient.
